SVC_RACE_DEFAULT.name: RACE — Airoha vendor (default UUIDs)
SVC_RACE_DEFAULT.description: RACE is Airoha’s vendor protocol tunneled over a single BLE GATT service: host writes command frames to TX and receives replies on RX (NOTIFY). It is the same surface tools such as race-toolkit use for provisioning, debug, and flash-style operations (see CVE-2025-20700 / CVE-2025-20702 disclosure). UUID matches race-toolkit UuidTable.AIROHA_GATT_SERVICE_UUID.

CHR_RACE_TX_DEFAULT.name: RACE TX — host → device
CHR_RACE_TX_DEFAULT.description: Outgoing leg: application writes RACE command packets here (host → firmware). Pair with RACE RX for NOTIFY responses. UUID matches AIROHA_GATT_TX_UUID in race-toolkit.

structure.description: Minimal RACE REQUEST: 05 5A | u16 LE length (payload octets + 2; here only the command word, so 2) | u16 LE command 0x1E08 | no extra payload octets beyond the LE command in the LENGTH field semantics used by race-toolkit. NOTIFY on RX carries RESPONSE 0x5B plus body (return_code plus vendor/build string).

RACE REQUEST frame: 05 5A | u16 LE (payload length + 2) | u16 LE command 0x0403 | 6-octet payload: storage selector u8 | high page-size selector u8 (0x01 = 256-byte page) | flash address u32 LE. Wire example 055A08000304000100001008 asks for 256 bytes from physical address 0x08100000; firmware replies on RX with STORAGE_PAGE_READ data.

CHR_RACE_RX_DEFAULT.name: RACE RX — device → host
CHR_RACE_RX_DEFAULT.description: Return leg: firmware sends RACE response frames via NOTIFY after handling a TX write. Enable NOTIFY before writing TX. UUID matches AIROHA_GATT_RX_UUID in race-toolkit.

SVC_RACE_SONY.name: RACE — Airoha vendor (Sony layout)
SVC_RACE_SONY.description: Sony version. RACE is Airoha’s vendor protocol tunneled over a single BLE GATT service: host writes command frames to TX and receives replies on RX (NOTIFY). It is the same surface tools such as race-toolkit use for provisioning, debug, and flash-style operations (see CVE-2025-20700 / CVE-2025-20702 disclosure). This UUID is the Sony OEM service mapping in race-toolkit (SONY_GATT_SERVICE_UUID).

CHR_RACE_TX_SONY.name: RACE TX (Sony) — host → device
CHR_RACE_TX_SONY.description: Sony version. Outgoing leg: application writes RACE command packets here (host → firmware). Pair with RACE RX (Sony) for NOTIFY responses. UUID matches SONY_GATT_TX_UUID in race-toolkit.

NOTIFY on RX carries RESPONSE 0x5B plus body (return_code plus vendor/build string).

RACE REQUEST frame: 05 5A | u16 LE (payload length + 2) | u16 LE command 0x0403 | 6-octet payload: storage selector u8 | high page-size selector u8 (0x01 = 256-byte page) | flash address u32 LE. Wire example 055A08000304000100001008 asks for 256 bytes from physical address 0x08100000; firmware replies on RX with STORAGE_PAGE_READ data.

CHR_RACE_RX_SONY.name: RACE RX (Sony) — device → host
CHR_RACE_RX_SONY.description: Sony version. Return leg: firmware sends RACE response frames via NOTIFY after handling a TX write. Enable NOTIFY before writing RACE TX (Sony). UUID matches SONY_GATT_RX_UUID in race-toolkit.

SVC_RACE_TRSPX.name: RACE — Airoha vendor (ISS/TRSPX app UUIDs)
SVC_RACE_TRSPX.description: TRSPX / ISS app UUID version. RACE is Airoha’s vendor protocol tunneled over a single BLE GATT service: host writes command frames to TX and receives replies on RX (NOTIFY). It is the same surface tools such as race-toolkit use for provisioning, debug, and flash-style operations (see CVE-2025-20700 / CVE-2025-20702 disclosure). This UUID is the TRSPX service mapping in race-toolkit (TRSPX_GATT_SERVICE_UUID; ISS-style vendor layout).

CHR_RACE_TX_TRSPX.name: RACE TX (TRSPX) — host → device
CHR_RACE_TX_TRSPX.description: TRSPX version. Outgoing leg: application writes RACE command packets here (host → firmware). Pair with RACE RX (TRSPX) for NOTIFY responses. UUID matches TRSPX_GATT_TX_UUID in race-toolkit.

NOTIFY on RX carries RESPONSE 0x5B plus body (return_code plus vendor/build string).

RACE REQUEST frame: 05 5A | u16 LE (payload length + 2) | u16 LE command 0x0403 | 6-octet payload: storage selector u8 | high page-size selector u8 (0x01 = 256-byte page) | flash address u32 LE. Wire example 055A08000304000100001008 asks for 256 bytes from physical address 0x08100000; firmware replies on RX with STORAGE_PAGE_READ data.

CHR_RACE_RX_TRSPX.name: RACE RX (TRSPX) — device → host
CHR_RACE_RX_TRSPX.description: TRSPX version. Return leg: firmware sends RACE response frames via NOTIFY after handling a TX write. Enable NOTIFY before writing RACE TX (TRSPX). UUID matches TRSPX_GATT_RX_UUID in race-toolkit.
